title: Quarto Coverage Tracking
text:
1 Documentation Home
This Quarto book documents the coverage-tracking feature described in the repository README.md.
Use the chapters in this book to track implementation decisions, scope, and progress.

title: Quarto Coverage Tracking
text:
1.1 Current chapters

spec.qmd: functional and technical specification for the first implementation.
SPEC-0.0.2-v001.qmd: versioned spec (0.0.2, pseudo-v001) for professor CSV editor, inline toggles, and export modes.
GUIDE-FOR-CONTENT-EDITORS-AND-TEACHERS.qmd: setup and operations guide for instructors and editors.

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.1 Goal
Provide a portable coverage-tracking add-on for Quarto books so students can see what has been covered and professors can compare sections, edit coverage, and maintain data in human-friendly files.
The implementation is static-site friendly (GitHub Pages compatible) and easy to move into a larger yrPublish feature collection.

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.2 Scope

2.2.1 In scope

Section-level coverage keyed by page URL + HTML section ID.
Student and professor modes in one JavaScript/CSS add-on.
CSV-based coverage data (one file per rendered HTML page per class section; see file layout below).
Professor UI tools for selecting coverage and exporting CSV text.
Helper scripts for adding missing heading IDs and updating coverage CSVs.



2.2.2 Out of scope

Auth and server-side write APIs.
Hosted database dependency.
Real-time writes from browser directly into repository files.

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.3 Functional requirements

2.3.1 FR-1: Coverage identity

Coverage is keyed by:

URL (for example /chapter1.html)
HTML ID (for example sec-limits-review)
Class section ID (for example MATH101-01)




2.3.2 FR-2: Student section picker

On first visit, students must choose exactly one class section.
Content is blurred until selection is made.
Student selection is stored per browser tab/session (sessionStorage).
Deep links must still trigger picker gating when no section is selected.



2.3.3 FR-3: Student highlighting

Covered items are highlighted.
Missing coverage entry defaults to not covered.
Sidebar control shows selected section name and lets student reset/re-pick.



2.3.4 FR-4: Professor mode and navigation

Professor mode activates with ?prof or ?prof=true.
Professor chooser supports selecting zero or more sections using checkboxes.
Chooser is not auto-opened on navigation; opens when professor clicks sidebar controls.
Esc dismisses chooser without applying changes.
Internal links preserve prof query parameter.



2.3.5 FR-5: Professor sidebar controls

Sidebar top shows selected professor sections as color-coded entries.
Clicking any selected section entry re-opens chooser.
If none selected, show Choose section(s) entry.
Include Edit page coverage CSV entry to open CSV editor.



2.3.6 FR-5b: Professor inline coverage toggles

When professor mode is active and at least one class section is selected, the tracker shows per-section checkboxes next to each trackable heading (or chapter title span) in the page content, color-coded by section.
Toggling an inline checkbox updates in-memory coverage, refreshes highlights, and stays in sync with the CSV editor dialog when it is open.
Nested ids are logical overrides: if an outer tracked id is covered but a nested tracked id is not, the nested region is rendered as uncovered.
Outer-id toggles may cascade to nested ids:

checking outer id: auto-check all descendants when all are unchecked; for mixed descendants, show custom choice dialog (Check this section and all nested sections vs Only check this section)
unchecking outer id: auto-uncheck descendants when all checked or all unchecked; for mixed descendants, show custom choice dialog (Uncheck this section and all nested sections vs Only uncheck this section)
pressing Esc in the nested decision dialog cancels and applies no checkbox changes

Inline checkbox actions support undo/redo hotkeys in professor mode (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y, plus macOS Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z).



2.3.7 FR-6: Coverage data file format

coverage-config.json remains JSON and defines sections and colors (optional coverageDataDir per section).
Coverage data is stored under coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/ with one CSV per HTML page: yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv (see proposed file structure). The runtime loads only the current page’s files.
CSV row formats:

URL,html-id,covered
URL,html-id,not-covered
URL,html-id (defaults to covered)
URL,html-id, (defaults to covered)

If (URL,html-id) is missing from the relevant page’s CSV for that section, default is not covered.



2.3.8 FR-7: Professor CSV editing UI

Professor can open page-level coverage editor in the UI.
Editor supports:

a single multi-line text-editor-style control for all rows on the page
one visible CSV row per trackable html-id, with the checkbox aligned in a gutter directly next to that row text
tri-state row status cycle: covered -> explicit not-covered -> implicit not-covered (omitted from CSV output)
set all page IDs to covered
set all page IDs to explicit not-covered
set all page IDs to implicit not-covered
convert all not-covered rows to explicit form
convert all not-covered rows to implicit form
undo/redo editor history (buttons + keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y, recognized while the dialog is open)
copy CSV text
download CSV file (filename aligned with yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv naming)

Implicit rows are visually grey/italic with a greyed checkbox, but still visible so users can promote them to explicit rows.
Manual edits to a row make it explicit; deleting/emptying a row line reverts that row to implicit not-covered (grey/italic) rather than removing it from the editor surface.
The dialog is draggable and non-modal; underlying page content remains scrollable while editing, and it is closed via an upper-right × button.
Workflow remains static-first: professor can copy/download and save offline into the corresponding repo file.



2.3.9 FR-7b: Trackable HTML ids (idsOnPage)

The runtime collects the same set of ids for highlighting, inline toggles, and the editor. In Quarto HTML books this typically includes elements with ids on section wrappers and chapter title h1 span[id] nodes, not only h1[id] / h2[id] on the heading element itself.



2.3.10 FR-8: Helper scripts

tools/yrAddSectionsIds.sh

input: one .qmd file
output: new .qmd with missing heading IDs inserted
skips fenced code blocks so # inside code is ignored

tools/yrUpdateCoverage.sh

input: section id, book root, data dir, one or more .qmd files
output: per-page yr-coverage--<page-slug>.updated.csv next to each target yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv
adds missing (URL,html-id) rows as not-covered

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.4 Proposed file structure
assets/coverage-tracking/
  coverage-config.json
  coverage-data/
    <SECTION-ID>/
      yr-coverage--index.html.csv
      yr-coverage--chapter1.html.csv
      yr-coverage--part-a--chapter.html.csv
  coverage-tracker.js
  coverage-tracker.css
  coverage-tracker.html
tools/
  yrAddSectionsIds.sh
  yrUpdateCoverage.sh

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.5 CSV semantics
Example section file:
URL,html-id,covered
/index.html,sec-how-to-use,covered
/index.html,sec-reading-plan,not-covered
/chapter1.html,sec-limits-review,
Interpretation:

Third column covered => covered
Third column not-covered => not covered
Missing/blank third column => covered
Missing row entirely => not covered

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.6 Sample demo book
The sample implementation is in sampleBooks/sample1/ and demonstrates:

student picker and highlighting
professor compare mode
professor sidebar controls
professor inline per-section checkboxes in page content (when at least one section is selected)
professor CSV editor/export workflow (tri-state per-line rows and inline CSV text editing)
per-page CSV coverage loading (lazy fetch per page)

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.7 Risks and mitigations

Missing stable section IDs:

mitigate with yrAddSectionsIds.sh and explicit {#id} authoring practice.

Inconsistent URLs between source and rendered pages:

mitigate with conventions in tooling and editor guide.

Theme collisions:

mitigate with namespaced .ct-* CSS selectors.

title: 2  Coverage Tracking Spec
text:
2.8 Definition of done

Coverage runtime reads coverage-config.json + per-page CSV files for the current section(s).
Student mode enforces picker gating and tab-scoped persistence.
Professor mode supports comparison, navigation persistence, sidebar controls, and chooser dismiss-on-Esc.
Professor can generate/export CSV coverage from UI.
Tools scripts exist and are documented.
Docs include setup and operations guide for editors/professors.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.1 Document purpose
This document specifies version 0.0.2 of the coverage-tracking feature. The pseudo-version v001 allows multiple revisions of this spec to coexist in the Quarto docs book (e.g. SPEC-0.0.2-v002.qmd later) without replacing prior text.
Audience: implementers and technical editors; professors who need to know how professor-mode editing and export behave.
Scope (v0.0.2): behavior of the “Edit page coverage CSV” dialog, live CSV updates, inline per-section checkboxes in professor mode, and tri-state per-line CSV serialization semantics.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.2 Relationship to other docs







File
Role




spec.qmd
Living consolidated specification; may evolve.


GUIDE-FOR-CONTENT-EDITORS-AND-TEACHERS.qmd
Setup, file paths, and day-to-day workflows.


This file
Frozen snapshot for 0.0.2 + v001: professor editing UX and export semantics.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.3 Persistent data

Config: coverage-config.json (JSON) — class sections, labels, colors, optional coverageDataDir override per section.
Coverage (option A — per HTML page, per class section): one CSV file per rendered page per section, using a fixed directory and filename pattern (large-book friendly; lazy-load one file per page in the browser).


3.3.1 Directory and filename convention
For each class section id <SECTION-ID> and canonical page URL path <PAGE> (e.g. /chapter1.html or /part-a/chapter.html):

Directory: assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/
Filename: yr-coverage--<PAGE-SLUG>.csv

<PAGE-SLUG> is derived from <PAGE> by:

Strip a leading /.
Replace every / with -- (so nested paths stay in one filename segment).

Examples:








Canonical page URL
PAGE-SLUG
Filename




/index.html
index.html
yr-coverage--index.html.csv


/chapter1.html
chapter1.html
yr-coverage--chapter1.html.csv


/part-a/chapter.html
part-a--chapter.html
yr-coverage--part-a--chapter.html.csv



Optional per-section override in JSON: "coverageDataDir": "assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/MATH101-01/" (must resolve to the section’s directory; trailing slash optional). If omitted, the implementation uses assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/.


3.3.2 Runtime loading

On each page load, the tracker fetches only the CSV files for the current canonical page URL—one request per class section (parallel fetch). No single multi-megabyte whole-book CSV is required in memory.
Navigating to another HTML page loads a new set of per-page files (full page navigation reloads the script; same-origin fetch applies).



3.3.3 Row format (unchanged)
Per-file content still uses the same row semantics as the global format; each file typically contains rows whose URL column matches that page (and may repeat the same URL on every row for clarity).


3.3.4 CSV row semantics (normative)



Row shape
Meaning




No row for (URL, html-id)
Not covered (default).


URL,html-id
Covered (short form; third column omitted).


URL,html-id,
Covered (empty third column).


URL,html-id,covered
Covered (explicit).


URL,html-id,not-covered
Not covered (explicit).



Optional header row URL,html-id,covered may be present; parsers should skip it when detected.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.4 Runtime state (in-memory)
After loading CSVs, the implementation holds a structure equivalent to:
sectionId → { pageUrl → { htmlId → boolean } }
where boolean is true iff that (URL, html-id) is covered under the rules above.
Static sites cannot write to Git; export is copy/download. Persisting changes is an offline editor workflow.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.5 URL and page identity

pageUrl: canonical first column for the current HTML page (e.g. /chapter1.html). Use one shared implementation: canonical path + getPathCandidates() (or equivalent) so merging matches CSV keys.
idsOnPage: ordered list of HTML id values that are editable in coverage UI on this page.

Normative: idsOnPage must be the same set used for:

student/professor highlighting,
the modal editor row list,
inline professor checkboxes.

If the DOM query for idsOnPage is narrower than the IDs used for highlights (e.g. headings in #quarto-content vs IDs only in margin TOC), the editor and CSV preview can omit rows that still drive colors — the section dropdown will then appear not to change the CSV. v0.0.2 requires a single definition of trackable IDs (see below).

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.6 Canonical idsOnPage (v0.0.2)
Implementations must define how IDs are collected, e.g.:

In Quarto book HTML, section wrappers often carry the anchor id (with h2 inside without a duplicate id), and chapter titles may use h1 span[id]. The runtime should treat those elements as the trackable anchors so idsOnPage matches getElementById / CSV keys.
Policy for elements outside #quarto-content (e.g. h2#toc-title in the margin TOC): include everywhere or exclude everywhere — choose one and document it in the editor guide.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.7 Professor mode: class section selection

Activated with ?prof or ?prof=true.
Professor selects zero or more class sections (checkbox chooser).
Sidebar shows color-coded entries; internal links preserve prof.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.8 Goals (v0.0.2)

3.8.1 Modal: “Edit page coverage CSV”

Class section dropdown — Changing selection reloads row state from in-memory coverage for (selectedSectionId, pageUrl, htmlId) and immediately refreshes highlights and export output.
Single editor surface, one row per html-id — The dialog uses one multi-line text-editor-like control; each idsOnPage entry is one CSV row with a checkbox aligned in a gutter beside that row.
Tri-state checkbox per row — Covered -> explicit not-covered -> implicit not-covered (grey/italic row and greyed checkbox). Implicit rows are omitted from exported CSV text.
Bulk row actions — The editor provides set-all covered / explicit not-covered / implicit not-covered plus conversions between explicit/implicit not-covered states.
Undo/redo — The editor keeps per-section history and supports undo/redo via UI buttons and keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y, with macOS Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z).
No separate Build action — Any row toggle or row-text edit updates in-memory state immediately.



3.8.2 Inline: per-heading, per-section controls
When professor mode is active and N ≥ 1 class sections are selected:

For each htmlId in idsOnPage and each selected sectionId, show one checkbox (or a grouped row of N), color-coded to coverage-config.json color for that section.
Toggle updates in-memory state, refreshes highlights, and any open CSV preview/export for that section.
Nested-id logic:

uncovered nested ids visually override covered ancestors
outer-id toggles may cascade to descendants
mixed descendant states use a custom draggable choice dialog with explicit action labels (not browser-native confirm dialogs)
Esc in that choice dialog cancels and applies no checkbox changes

Inline actions support undo/redo hotkeys while professor mode is active (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y, and macOS equivalents).



3.8.3 Export semantics with tri-state rows

Export writes header URL,html-id,covered.
Export includes explicit rows only:

explicit covered rows (e.g. URL,html-id,covered, or user-edited equivalent)
explicit not-covered rows (e.g. URL,html-id,not-covered, or user-edited equivalent)

Export omits implicit not-covered rows.
Users can still reach former “maximize” behavior by making all rows explicit, and former “minimize” behavior by leaving many rows implicit.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.9 Row-state details (normative)

Covered (explicit): row is included; checkbox appears checked.
Not-covered (explicit): row is included with explicit non-covered semantics.
Not-covered (implicit): row is visible but grey/italic and omitted from exported CSV.
If a user begins editing an implicit row text manually, that row becomes explicit.
If a user deletes/empties a row line, that row reverts to implicit (grey/italic) rather than disappearing from the editor.
The editor is draggable and non-modal so the underlying page can still be scrolled and read during editing.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.10 Architecture (layers)

Load — for the current page only, fetch yr-coverage--<PAGE-SLUG>.csv under each section directory (parallel).
Parse — build sectionId → pageUrl → htmlId → boolean (same structure as before; only current page keys populated from network).
Render highlights — existing overlap behavior with inline checkbox controls.
Edit — modal and inline toggles write the same in-memory state.
Export — serialize explicit rows; copy/download.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.11 Accessibility

Checkboxes: aria-label including section name and heading id where helpful.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.12 Implementation checklist

idsOnPage query matches highlight coverage IDs (section ids + chapter title span ids; TOC excluded).
pageUrl + merge logic shared everywhere.
Editor: change on section <select> keeps row state synced for previous and new section.
Editor: one row per idsOnPage item with inline checkbox + editable CSV line text.
Editor: tri-state row cycle (covered / explicit not-covered / implicit not-covered).
Inline checkbox component bound to same state as modal.
Export serializes header + explicit rows; copy; download.
Sample: two sections, three pages, multiple ids per page — section dropdown and per-row editor state reflect each section.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
section: 3.13 Open decisions (record in project when resolved)
text:
3.13 Open decisions (record in project when resolved)

Whole-book export in UI: keep current-page only, or add multi-page export later.
Include or exclude toc-title (and similar) in idsOnPage consistently.
If modal and inline editor are both visible: sync rules or single source of truth.

title: 3  Coverage tracking — Specification v0.0.2 (pseudo-v001)
text:
3.14 Changelog (this file)

v0.0.2 / v001: Professor CSV editor (live updates, unified idsOnPage), inline per-section checkboxes, tri-state per-line CSV editing semantics.
v0.0.2 / v001 (revision): Per-page CSV layout option A: coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv, lazy load per page, optional coverageDataDir.

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.1 Who this guide is for
This guide is for people who maintain Quarto books and track class coverage.
Students do not need special documentation to consume the book.
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.2 What this system does

Students pick a class section and see covered content highlighted.
Professors can compare multiple sections in ?prof mode.
Coverage data is maintained in per-class-section directories, with one CSV per rendered HTML page (see naming below).
Professors can generate updated CSV content from the website UI.
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.3 Required directory structure
In your Quarto book project, include:
assets/coverage-tracking/
  coverage-config.json
  coverage-data/
    <SECTION-ID>/
      yr-coverage--index.html.csv
      yr-coverage--chapter1.html.csv
      yr-coverage--part-a--chapter.html.csv
  coverage-tracker.js
  coverage-tracker.css
  coverage-tracker.html
Filename rule: yr-coverage-- + slug + .csv, where slug is the page path without a leading slash, with / replaced by -- (e.g. /chapter1.html → chapter1.html; /part-a/chapter.html → part-a--chapter.html).
Optional helper scripts:
tools/
  yrAddSectionsIds.sh
  yrUpdateCoverage.sh
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.4 File formats

4.4.1 coverage-config.json (JSON, required)
Defines available sections and styling.
{
  "sections": [
    {
      "id": "MATH101-01",
      "label": "MATH101 Section 01",
      "color": "#2e7d32"
    }
  ]
}
Optional: "coverageDataDir": "assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/MATH101-01/" if the section directory is not the default coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/.


4.4.2 coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv (CSV, required per page you track)
Each class section has its own directory. Each rendered page you care about has its own CSV file in that directory. Large books stay maintainable: you edit only the files for the pages you change.
Accepted row formats:

URL,html-id,covered
URL,html-id,not-covered
URL,html-id
URL,html-id,

Default semantics:

Missing row => not covered
Blank/missing third column => covered

Example:
URL,html-id,covered
/chapter1.html,sec-limits-review,covered
/chapter1.html,sec-continuity-basics,not-covered
/chapter1.html,sec-derivatives-preview,
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.5 Quarto integration steps
In your book _quarto.yml:

Include coverage config and CSV directory as project resources.
Include coverage CSS.
Include the tracker script HTML include.

Example:
project:
  type: book
  resources:
    - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-config.json
    - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data/

format:
  html:
    css:
      - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-tracker.css
    include-after-body:
      - assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-tracker.html
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.6 Authoring content safely
Coverage is based on HTML IDs, so section headings should have explicit IDs:
## Limits Review {#sec-limits-review}
This makes coverage stable and portable across edits.
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.7 Helper tools

4.7.1 yrAddSectionsIds.sh
Purpose:

add missing heading IDs in a .qmd file
skip fenced code blocks

Usage:
./tools/yrAddSectionsIds.sh input.qmd [output.qmd]


4.7.2 yrUpdateCoverage.sh
Purpose:

For a given class section, update one per-page CSV per .qmd file.
Adds missing (URL,html-id) rows as not-covered.
Writes yr-coverage--<page-slug>.updated.csv next to each target yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv.

Usage (from your Quarto book root, so URLs resolve to /index.html, /chapter1.html, etc.):
./tools/yrUpdateCoverage.sh --section MATH101-01 --data-dir assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data index.qmd chapter1.qmd
From another working directory, pass --book-root pointing at the book project:
./tools/yrUpdateCoverage.sh --section MATH101-01 --book-root path/to/book --data-dir path/to/book/assets/coverage-tracking/coverage-data path/to/book/index.qmd
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
text:
4.8 Professor workflow in UI

4.8.1 Compare mode

Open book with ?prof or ?prof=true.
Use sidebar entry to open section chooser.
Select zero or more sections with checkboxes.
Apply and review highlighted overlap.



4.8.2 Inline toggles (in page content)

After at least one class section is selected, each trackable heading on the page shows small checkboxes (one per selected section), color-coded by section.
Toggling an inline checkbox updates highlights immediately and stays consistent with the CSV editor dialog if it is open.
Nested IDs are respected:

if an outer id is covered but a nested tracked id is not, the nested region is rendered as uncovered
checking/unchecking an outer id can cascade to nested ids
for mixed nested states, a custom draggable decision dialog appears with explicit choices (for example, Check this section and all nested sections vs Only check this section)
pressing Esc on that decision dialog cancels and applies no checkbox changes

Outside the editor dialog, professor inline checkbox actions support undo/redo hotkeys (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y, and Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z on macOS).



4.8.3 Edit page coverage CSV

In professor mode, click Edit page coverage CSV in sidebar.
Choose the class section to edit.
The dialog uses a single multi-line text-editor-style control. Each HTML id maps to one row, with a checkbox aligned in a left gutter next to that row text.
Checkbox state is tri-state per row:

checked = explicit covered
unchecked = explicit not-covered
grey/italic (indeterminate) = implicit not-covered (line is omitted from CSV output)

Use:

Undo / Redo (or keyboard Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y; Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z on macOS)
Set all covered
Set all explicit not-covered
Set all implicit not-covered
Make all not-covereds explicit
Make all not-covereds implicit
individual row toggles (cycle through covered → explicit not-covered → implicit not-covered)

If a line is edited to empty/deleted, that row does not disappear; it reverts to implicit not-covered (grey/italic).
You can manually edit any row text. If you start editing an implicit (grey) row, it becomes explicit (not grey) so that row is included in output.
The dialog is draggable and non-modal, so you can move it and scroll/read the underlying page while editing. Use the × button in the top-right of the dialog to close it.
Undo/redo hotkeys are recognized while the dialog is open (not only when the text area is focused).
Copy / Download exports only explicit rows (plus header). Save/update the matching yr-coverage--<page-slug>.csv file in the repo under coverage-data/<SECTION-ID>/.



4.8.4 Heading IDs in Quarto HTML
Quarto often places the stable anchor on a <section id="..."> wrapper (with the visible heading inside), or on a <span id="..."> inside the chapter title h1. Use explicit {#id} on headings in source when possible, and ensure CSV html-id values match the rendered DOM ids (see the sample book).
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
section: 4.9 Notes on defaults and new content
text:
4.9 Notes on defaults and new content

If a new heading is added but not listed in CSV, it is interpreted as not covered.
This default is useful during course progression because coverage accumulates over time.
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers

title: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
section: 4.10 Moving this feature into another repository
text:
4.10 Moving this feature into another repository
To copy this feature into a larger Quarto feature collection:

Copy assets/coverage-tracking/.
Copy tools/ scripts if desired.
Update target book _quarto.yml.
Render and test student + professor modes.
crumbs: 4  Guide for Content Editors and Teachers
